creator: astralblue@usa.net
text:
The recent change in mtree(8)'s behavior (escaping filenames using
strvis(3)/strunvis(3)) opened two bugs.

Because the special characters '/', '*', '?' and '[' are checked before
the `spec' filename is strunvis(3)'ed, the corresponding metacharacters
(M-/, M-* and so on) are misinterpreted.  The misinterpretation of M-/
triggers the no-embedded-slash check and chokes mtree -d to death, and
others make mtree -d fnmatch() filenames against the spec while it
should not.

Fix: 

Apply the attached patch from /usr.  Note that this patch is
backward-compatible with the previous strvis/strunvis version, since
strunvis() understands both old and new format.

-- 
Eugene M. Kim <astralblue@usa.net>

"Is your music unpopular?  Make it popular; make music
which people like, or make people who like your music."

How-To-Repeat: 
Make files with offending characters in their name and run ``mtree |
mtree -d'' in the directory.  mtree -d will die upon a filename with 
M-/.
